content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of positive integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč. You may change up to two elements of this sequence, each of them to any positive integer (not necessarily the same for two elements). Let's denote the resulting sequence by b1,b2,‚Ä¶,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,bN‚Äč.

Consider all 2N2^N2N subsequences of b1,b2,‚Ä¶,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,bN‚Äč. For each subsequence, compute the sum of its elements (000 for the empty subsequence). Let's denote the set of these sums by SSS; note that a set contains each integer at most once. Finally, NOSS is the size of set SSS. For example, if [b1,b2,b3]=[17,23,40][b_1, b_2, b_3] = [17, 23, 40][b1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,b3‚Äč]=[17,23,40], then S={0,17,23,40,57,63,80}S = \{0, 17, 23, 40, 57, 63, 80\}S={0,17,23,40,57,63,80} and NOSS is 777.

Find the maximum possible NOSS.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the maximum possible NOSS.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: One possible way is to change 333 to 101010 and 555 to 202020. Then S={0‚Ä¶7,10‚Ä¶17,20‚Ä¶27,30‚Ä¶37}S = \{0 \ldots 7, 10 \ldots 17, 20 \ldots 27, 30 \ldots 37\}S={0‚Ä¶7,10‚Ä¶17,20‚Ä¶27,30‚Ä¶37} (a‚Ä¶ba \ldots ba‚Ä¶b denotes all integer from aaa to bbb inclusive) and NOSS is 323232.
Example case 2: One possible way is to change one element of the initial sequence from 111 to 101010 and another from 111 to 202020.
Example case 3: The optimal NOSS is achieved even if nothing is changed.
